mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in real retail sales, measured by RSAFS, indicates sustained consumer spending power, which drives corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between monthly RSAFS growth and 20-day forward returns remains below 0.05 or negative over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above a key threshold signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which historically precede recessions and equity market declines. When UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, it triggers negative forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: After trigger, S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of occurrences, or average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated Federal Funds rate combined with declining housing starts indicates that monetary tightening is actively slowing the housing sector, a key driver of economic activity. This joint condition produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and HOUST declines month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: The joint signal yields positive 20-day returns in more than 40% of cases, or the negative signal accuracy is below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime, higher Federal Funds rates compress equity valuations due to rising discount rates and reduced liquidity. The current tightening cycle implies that continued elevated rates will depress S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: During tightening periods (defined by Fed rate hikes), the correlation between FEDFUNDS level and subsequent 20-day returns is positive (i.e., higher rates associated with positive returns).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production signals robust economic output and corporate profitability, which supports higher equity prices. Positive trends in INDPRO are a reliable indicator of positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between 3-month INDPRO growth and subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns is less than 0.05 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts, a leading indicator of economic activity, falling below a critical level signals residential investment contraction and broader economic weakness, leading to negative equity market returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: After HOUST falls below 1400, S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the economy is at full employment (UNRATE below 5%) and retail sales growth is accelerating above 2% month-over-month, it signals overheating that may provoke tighter monetary policy, ultimately leading to equity market declines. This interaction between labor and demand data creates a negative forward return signal.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 5% and RSAFS month-over-month growth above 2%
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of cases, or the average return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: During an economic expansion phase, strong payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) reinforces expectations of robust consumer spending and earnings, leading to positive short-term equity market returns. The regime of expansion amplifies the bullish signal from payrolls.
failure_condition: In expansion regimes, the 5-day return following above-median payroll growth is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).
